vocab.meaning: vội vã, chạy đua với thời gian
vocab.example: I always wear a watch when I'm working against the clock to meet a deadline, as it helps me stay focused.

vocab.meaning: rất đắt đỏ, tốn nhiều tiền
vocab.example: My father's collection of Swiss watches must have cost a pretty penny, but they are incredibly durable.
To be honest, I only wear a formal dress watch once in a blue moon, usually just for weddings or galas.

vocab.meaning: có đầy đủ các tính năng hiện đại (đôi khi không cần thiết)
vocab.example: I prefer a simple analog face over a smartwatch that has all the bells and whistles but distracting notifications.

vocab.word: turn back the hands of time
vocab.example: Wearing this vintage watch makes me feel like I can turn back the hands of time to a more elegant era.

vocab.meaning: đều đặn, chính xác như một bộ máy
vocab.example: I’ve had this Seiko for ten years and it still runs like clockwork without ever needing a repair.

vocab.meaning: vừa kịp lúc (ngay trước khi quá muộn)
vocab.example: I checked my watch and realized I had to run; luckily, I reached the boarding gate in the nick of time.

vocab.meaning: thời gian là cốt yếu/vô cùng quan trọng
vocab.example: In my line of work, time is of the essence, so having a reliable timepiece on my wrist is non-negotiable.
Without a watch, I tend to lose track of time when I'm engrossed in a good book at the park.

vocab.word: at the drop of a hat
vocab.meaning: ngay lập tức, không do dự
vocab.example: If I could afford a Patek Philippe, I would buy it at the drop of a hat because of its investment value.
